description: Movie-RAG evaluation dataset. 200 reviewed Q/A pairs across five categories and three difficulty levels. ground_truth_movies contains the exact movie titles considered relevant for retrieval metrics.

items.question: What films did Quentin Tarantino direct?
items.ground_truth_answer: Quentin Tarantino directed Reservoir Dogs, Pulp Fiction, Jackie Brown, the Kill Bill films, Inglourious Basterds, Django Unchained, The Hateful Eight, and Once Upon a Time in Hollywood.
ground_truth_movies: Kill Bill: The Whole Bloody Affair
Once Upon a Time... in Hollywood

items.question: List the feature films directed by Christopher Nolan.
items.ground_truth_answer: Christopher Nolan directed Memento, Insomnia, Batman Begins, The Prestige, The Dark Knight, Inception, The Dark Knight Rises, Interstellar, Dunkirk, Tenet, and Oppenheimer.

items.question: Which movies did Martin Scorsese direct?
items.ground_truth_answer: Martin Scorsese directed Taxi Driver, Raging Bull, Goodfellas, Casino, Gangs of New York, The Aviator, The Departed, Shutter Island, The Wolf of Wall Street, and The Irishman, among others.

items.question: What films has Steven Spielberg directed?
items.ground_truth_answer: Steven Spielberg directed Jaws, E.T., the Indiana Jones films, Jurassic Park, Schindler's List, Saving Private Ryan, Minority Report, Catch Me If You Can, and Lincoln, among many others.
ground_truth_movies: Close Encounters of the Third Kind
Indiana Jones and the Temple of Doom
Indiana Jones and the Last Crusade
Indiana Jones and the Kingdom of the Crystal Skull

items.question: Which films did David Fincher direct?
items.ground_truth_answer: David Fincher directed Se7en, Fight Club, Zodiac, The Social Network, The Girl with the Dragon Tattoo, Gone Girl, and Mank, among others.
ground_truth_movies: The Curious Case of Benjamin Button
The Girl with the Dragon Tattoo

items.question: List the films directed by Denis Villeneuve.
items.ground_truth_answer: Denis Villeneuve directed Incendies, Prisoners, Enemy, Sicario, Arrival, Blade Runner 2049, Dune, and Dune: Part Two.

items.question: What movies did Wes Anderson direct?
items.ground_truth_answer: Wes Anderson directed Rushmore, The Royal Tenenbaums, The Life Aquatic, Fantastic Mr. Fox, Moonrise Kingdom, The Grand Budapest Hotel, and Isle of Dogs, among others.
ground_truth_movies: The Life Aquatic with Steve Zissou
The French Dispatch of the Liberty, Kansas Evening Sun
The Wonderful Story of Henry Sugar

items.question: Which films did the Coen brothers direct?
items.ground_truth_answer: Joel and Ethan Coen directed Fargo, The Big Lebowski, No Country for Old Men, True Grit, A Serious Man, Inside Llewyn Davis, and Burn After Reading, among others.

items.question: What films has Bong Joon-ho directed?
items.ground_truth_answer: Bong Joon-ho directed Memories of Murder, The Host, Mother, Snowpiercer, Okja, and Parasite.

items.question: List the films directed by Paul Thomas Anderson.
items.ground_truth_answer: Paul Thomas Anderson directed Boogie Nights, Magnolia, There Will Be Blood, The Master, Inherent Vice, Phantom Thread, and Licorice Pizza.

items.question: Which movies did Stanley Kubrick direct?
items.ground_truth_answer: Stanley Kubrick directed Dr. Strangelove, 2001: A Space Odyssey, A Clockwork Orange, Barry Lyndon, The Shining, Full Metal Jacket, and Eyes Wide Shut.

items.question: Who directed Inception?
items.ground_truth_answer: Christopher Nolan directed Inception.

items.ground_truth_answer: Pulp Fiction was released in 1994.

items.ground_truth_answer: Brad Pitt played Tyler Durden in Fight Club.

items.ground_truth_answer: Bong Joon-ho directed Parasite.

items.ground_truth_answer: Leonardo DiCaprio plays Cobb in Inception.

items.question: Who directed Blade Runner 2049?
items.ground_truth_answer: Denis Villeneuve directed Blade Runner 2049.

items.question: Movies about dreams within dreams
items.ground_truth_answer: Inception is the canonical film about dreams within dreams, where characters enter nested layers of shared dreaming.

items.question: Films about isolation and survival in space
items.ground_truth_answer: The Martian, Gravity, Interstellar, and 2001: A Space Odyssey all explore human isolation and survival in space.

items.question: Movies that explore memory loss and fractured identity
items.ground_truth_answer: Memento, Eternal Sunshine of the Spotless Mind, and Total Recall all center on memory loss and the way it disrupts personal identity.
ground_truth_movies: Eternal Sunshine of the Spotless Mind

items.question: Movies about class struggle and economic inequality
items.ground_truth_answer: Parasite, Snowpiercer, and Joker each use the divide between rich and poor as a central engine of their conflict.

items.question: Crime films told in non-linear chronological order
items.ground_truth_answer: Pulp Fiction, Memento, and Reservoir Dogs all deliberately fracture chronological order to reframe their crime narratives.

items.question: Movies about artificial intelligence developing consciousness
items.ground_truth_answer: Ex Machina, Her, Blade Runner 2049, and The Matrix all grapple with AI that becomes — or appears to become — conscious.

items.question: Heist movies with elaborate multi-step plans
items.ground_truth_answer: Ocean's Eleven, Inception, and Heat all feature carefully choreographed, multi-step heists as their narrative spine.

items.question: What other films has the director of Inception made?
items.ground_truth_answer: Christopher Nolan also directed The Dark Knight, Interstellar, The Prestige, Memento, Dunkirk, and Tenet, among others.

items.question: Which movies feature both Leonardo DiCaprio and Tom Hardy?
items.ground_truth_answer: Leonardo DiCaprio and Tom Hardy appeared together in Inception and The Revenant.

items.question: Other films by Denis Villeneuve besides Dune
items.ground_truth_answer: Beyond Dune, Denis Villeneuve directed Arrival, Blade Runner 2049, Sicario, and Prisoners.

items.question: Films where Brad Pitt worked with director David Fincher
items.ground_truth_answer: Brad Pitt and David Fincher collaborated on Se7en, Fight Club, and The Curious Case of Benjamin Button.
ground_truth_movies: The Curious Case of Benjamin Button

items.question: Other crime films directed by Martin Scorsese
items.ground_truth_answer: Martin Scorsese directed Goodfellas, The Departed, Casino, and The Irishman, among other crime films.

items.question: Movies that star both Robert De Niro and Al Pacino
items.ground_truth_answer: Robert De Niro and Al Pacino share the screen in Heat and The Irishman (and technically appear in The Godfather Part II, though not in the same scenes).

items.question: How do Christopher Nolan's films differ in style from Denis Villeneuve's?
items.ground_truth_answer: Both make cerebral sci-fi, but Nolan tends toward intricately structured time-puzzle narratives (Inception, Tenet, Memento) while Villeneuve favors slower, more atmospheric, dread-laden compositions (Arrival, Blade Runner 2049, Sicario).

items.question: Compare the depiction of war in Saving Private Ryan and Dunkirk
items.ground_truth_answer: Saving Private Ryan depicts war through visceral, sustained combat focused on a small unit's mission, while Dunkirk uses a minimalist, near-dialogue-free, tri-timeline structure to convey the tension of evacuation rather than combat itself.

items.question: What do Parasite and Snowpiercer share thematically?
items.ground_truth_answer: Both are Bong Joon-ho films that use spatial verticality (a house's basement, a train's compartments) as a literal metaphor for class hierarchy and the violence of economic inequality.

items.question: What stylistic elements do Quentin Tarantino and the Coen Brothers share?
items.ground_truth_answer: Both are known for dialogue-driven scripts, sudden eruptions of violence punctuating dark humor, ensemble casts of eccentric characters, and genre pastiche — though Tarantino leans into homage and stylization while the Coens lean into regional Americana and dry absurdism.

items.question: How is Blade Runner 2049 connected to the original Blade Runner?
items.ground_truth_answer: Blade Runner 2049 is a direct sequel set decades after the original, with Denis Villeneuve replacing Ridley Scott as director. Both films are noir-inflected sci-fi exploring what separates humans from replicants, with 2049 expanding the universe and continuing Deckard's story.

items.question: Compare The Dark Knight and Joker as films about the Joker character
items.ground_truth_answer: The Dark Knight features the Joker as a chaos-agent antagonist in a crime epic centered on Batman, while Joker (2019) is a standalone character study of mental illness and societal neglect producing the same figure — different genres (crime thriller vs. psychological drama), different leads (Ledger vs. Phoenix), no shared continuity.

items.question: Who directed The Matrix?
items.ground_truth_answer: Lana Wachowski and Lilly Wachowski directed The Matrix.

items.question: Who directed The Godfather?
items.ground_truth_answer: Francis Ford Coppola directed The Godfather.

items.question: Movies where time travel creates dangerous consequences
items.ground_truth_answer: Twelve Monkeys, Looper, and Predestination use time travel to create paradoxes and dangerous consequences.

items.question: Films about discovering that reality is artificial or controlled
items.ground_truth_answer: The Matrix, The Truman Show, and eXistenZ center on protagonists discovering that their reality is constructed or controlled.

items.question: Dystopian movies about government surveillance and control
items.ground_truth_answer: Nineteen Eighty-Four, Brazil, Minority Report, and V for Vendetta depict authoritarian surveillance and social control.

items.question: Movies about surviving environmental collapse
items.ground_truth_answer: Snowpiercer, The Day After Tomorrow, WALL·E, and Interstellar portray societies confronting environmental catastrophe.

items.question: Films about robots learning empathy or humanity
items.ground_truth_answer: WALL·E, A.I. Artificial Intelligence, The Iron Giant, and Bicentennial Man explore machines developing empathy or human qualities.

items.question: Movies centered on grief after losing a family member
items.ground_truth_answer: Manchester by the Sea, Three Billboards Outside Ebbing, Missouri, Ordinary People, and Rabbit Hole examine family grief and guilt.

items.question: Films about artists driven toward self-destruction by perfectionism
items.ground_truth_answer: Whiplash, Black Swan, and Amadeus portray artistic ambition becoming obsession and self-destruction.

items.question: Thrillers with unreliable narrators and fractured perception
items.ground_truth_answer: Fight Club, Shutter Island, Memento, and The Usual Suspects use unreliable narration to destabilize the audience's understanding.

items.question: Westerns that question simple ideas of heroes and villains
items.ground_truth_answer: Unforgiven, No Country for Old Men, and True Grit present morally ambiguous violence rather than uncomplicated heroism.

items.question: Movies about strangers becoming a found family
items.ground_truth_answer: Guardians of the Galaxy, Little Miss Sunshine, and Hunt for the Wilderpeople follow mismatched people who form chosen families.

items.question: Coming-of-age films about identity and belonging
items.ground_truth_answer: Lady Bird, Boyhood, Moonlight, and Stand by Me examine young people forming identities and finding belonging.

items.question: Movies about journalists exposing powerful institutions
items.ground_truth_answer: Spotlight, All the President's Men, and The Post dramatize journalists investigating institutional wrongdoing.

items.question: Courtroom dramas about doubt and the meaning of justice
items.ground_truth_answer: 12 Angry Men, A Few Good Men, and Anatomy of a Fall use legal proceedings to question truth and justice.

items.question: Prison films about preserving hope and dignity
items.ground_truth_answer: The Shawshank Redemption, The Green Mile, and Escape from Alcatraz focus on prisoners preserving dignity under confinement.

items.question: Movies portraying addiction and psychological decline
items.ground_truth_answer: Requiem for a Dream, Trainspotting, and Leaving Las Vegas depict addiction's destructive physical and emotional consequences.

items.question: Films about loneliness in a crowded city
items.ground_truth_answer: Taxi Driver, Lost in Translation, and Her portray isolation despite dense urban surroundings.

items.question: Revenge movies where vengeance consumes the protagonist
items.ground_truth_answer: Oldboy, Kill Bill: Vol. 1, Gladiator, and John Wick center on protagonists whose lives become defined by revenge.

items.question: Psychological films about doubles and divided identity
items.ground_truth_answer: Enemy, Black Swan, and The Prestige use doubles or mirrored identities to explore obsession and fractured selves.

items.question: Science-fiction films about humanity's first contact with aliens
items.ground_truth_answer: Arrival, Contact, and Close Encounters of the Third Kind focus on communication during first contact.
ground_truth_movies: Close Encounters of the Third Kind

items.question: Movies about immigrant families balancing two cultures
items.ground_truth_answer: Minari, The Farewell, and Everything Everywhere All at Once explore immigrant families negotiating cultural identity.

items.question: Films about musicians sacrificing stability for their craft
items.ground_truth_answer: Whiplash, La La Land, and Sound of Metal examine the personal cost of musical ambition.

items.question: Lighthearted heist films built around an ensemble plan
items.ground_truth_answer: Ocean's Eleven, Logan Lucky, and The Italian Job combine elaborate ensemble robberies with humor.

items.question: Horror films where grief takes a supernatural form
items.ground_truth_answer: Hereditary, The Babadook, and Midsommar transform bereavement and family trauma into horror.

items.question: Movies about greed and corruption in high finance
items.ground_truth_answer: The Wolf of Wall Street, The Big Short, and Margin Call examine greed and systemic corruption in finance.

items.question: Films about media manipulation and manufactured narratives
items.ground_truth_answer: Network, The Truman Show, and Nightcrawler critique media systems that manipulate reality for audiences.

items.question: War films focused on soldiers' lasting psychological trauma
items.ground_truth_answer: Apocalypse Now, The Deer Hunter, and Full Metal Jacket emphasize war's psychological damage.

items.question: Road movies where an unlikely friendship develops
items.ground_truth_answer: Green Book, Thelma & Louise, and Rain Man use travel to develop transformative relationships.

items.question: Science-fiction films about artificial romantic companions
items.ground_truth_answer: Her, Blade Runner 2049, and Ex Machina explore intimacy between humans and artificial beings.

items.question: Survival films about isolation in the wilderness
items.ground_truth_answer: The Revenant, Into the Wild, and Cast Away center on individuals enduring isolation and hostile environments.

items.question: Movies where characters travel through multiple universes
items.ground_truth_answer: Everything Everywhere All at Once, Spider-Man: No Way Home, and Doctor Strange in the Multiverse of Madness use multiverse travel.
ground_truth_movies: Doctor Strange in the Multiverse of Madness

items.question: Movies where cooking becomes a form of creativity or identity
items.ground_truth_answer: Ratatouille, Chef, and The Menu use food and cooking to explore creativity, work, and identity.

items.question: Films where dreams blur the boundary with reality
items.ground_truth_answer: Inception, Mulholland Drive, and Paprika create uncertainty between dreams and waking reality.

items.question: Movies about ordinary people resisting authoritarian systems
items.ground_truth_answer: V for Vendetta, Brazil, and Nineteen Eighty-Four portray individuals resisting oppressive state control.

items.question: Which films pair director Steven Spielberg with actor Tom Hanks?
items.ground_truth_answer: Steven Spielberg directed Tom Hanks in Saving Private Ryan, Catch Me If You Can, The Terminal, and Bridge of Spies.

items.question: Which Martin Scorsese films star Leonardo DiCaprio?
items.ground_truth_answer: Their collaborations include Gangs of New York, The Aviator, The Departed, Shutter Island, and The Wolf of Wall Street.

items.question: Which Quentin Tarantino films feature Samuel L. Jackson?
items.ground_truth_answer: Samuel L. Jackson appears in Pulp Fiction, Jackie Brown, Kill Bill: Vol. 2, Django Unchained, and The Hateful Eight.

items.question: Which Christopher Nolan films star Christian Bale?
items.ground_truth_answer: Christian Bale stars in Batman Begins, The Prestige, The Dark Knight, and The Dark Knight Rises.

items.question: Which Denis Villeneuve films star Jake Gyllenhaal?
items.ground_truth_answer: Jake Gyllenhaal stars in Villeneuve's Prisoners and Enemy.

items.question: Which David Fincher films feature Rooney Mara?
items.ground_truth_answer: Rooney Mara appears in The Social Network and stars in The Girl with the Dragon Tattoo.
ground_truth_movies: The Girl with the Dragon Tattoo

items.question: Which Wes Anderson films feature Bill Murray?
items.ground_truth_answer: Bill Murray appears in Rushmore, The Royal Tenenbaums, The Life Aquatic with Steve Zissou, Moonrise Kingdom, The Grand Budapest Hotel, and Isle of Dogs.
ground_truth_movies: The Life Aquatic with Steve Zissou

items.question: Which Godfather films were directed by Francis Ford Coppola and star Al Pacino?
items.ground_truth_answer: The Godfather, The Godfather Part II, and The Godfather Part III were directed by Coppola and star Al Pacino.

items.question: Which Steven Spielberg films star Harrison Ford as Indiana Jones?
items.ground_truth_answer: They include Raiders of the Lost Ark, Indiana Jones and the Temple of Doom, Indiana Jones and the Last Crusade, and Indiana Jones and the Kingdom of the Crystal Skull.
ground_truth_movies: Indiana Jones and the Temple of Doom
Indiana Jones and the Last Crusade
Indiana Jones and the Kingdom of the Crystal Skull

items.question: Which Ridley Scott films star Russell Crowe?
items.ground_truth_answer: Their collaborations include Gladiator, A Good Year, American Gangster, and Robin Hood.

items.question: Which Coen brothers films feature Frances McDormand?
items.ground_truth_answer: Frances McDormand appears in Blood Simple, Fargo, The Man Who Wasn't There, Burn After Reading, and Hail, Caesar!

items.question: Which Paul Thomas Anderson films star Daniel Day-Lewis?
items.ground_truth_answer: Daniel Day-Lewis stars in There Will Be Blood and Phantom Thread.

items.question: Which Tim Burton films star Johnny Depp?
items.ground_truth_answer: Their collaborations include Edward Scissorhands, Ed Wood, Sleepy Hollow, Charlie and the Chocolate Factory, Sweeney Todd, Alice in Wonderland, and Dark Shadows.
ground_truth_movies: Sweeney Todd: The Demon Barber of Fleet Street

items.question: Which Martin Scorsese films star Robert De Niro?
items.ground_truth_answer: Their collaborations include Mean Streets, Taxi Driver, Raging Bull, GoodFellas, Cape Fear, Casino, The Irishman, and Killers of the Flower Moon.

items.question: Which Greta Gerwig films star Saoirse Ronan?
items.ground_truth_answer: Saoirse Ronan stars in Gerwig's Lady Bird and Little Women.

items.question: Which Damien Chazelle films star Ryan Gosling?
items.ground_truth_answer: Ryan Gosling stars in La La Land and First Man.

items.question: Which Bong Joon Ho films star Song Kang-ho?
items.ground_truth_answer: Song Kang-ho stars in Memories of Murder, The Host, Snowpiercer, and Parasite.

items.question: What major films were directed by Alfonso Cuarón?
items.ground_truth_answer: Alfonso Cuarón directed Children of Men, Gravity, Roma, and Harry Potter and the Prisoner of Azkaban.
ground_truth_movies: Harry Potter and the Prisoner of Azkaban

items.question: Which major films were directed by James Cameron?
items.ground_truth_answer: James Cameron directed The Terminator, Aliens, Terminator 2, Titanic, and Avatar.

items.question: Which films star both Tom Hanks and Meg Ryan?
items.ground_truth_answer: Tom Hanks and Meg Ryan co-star in Joe Versus the Volcano, Sleepless in Seattle, and You've Got Mail.

items.question: Which films star both Emma Stone and Ryan Gosling?
items.ground_truth_answer: They co-star in Crazy, Stupid, Love., Gangster Squad, and La La Land.

items.question: Which films star both Robert De Niro and Joe Pesci?
items.ground_truth_answer: They co-star in Raging Bull, GoodFellas, Casino, and The Irishman.

items.question: Which films feature both Matt Damon and Ben Affleck?
items.ground_truth_answer: They appear together in Good Will Hunting, Dogma, and The Last Duel.

items.question: Which films star both Kate Winslet and Leonardo DiCaprio?
items.ground_truth_answer: They co-star in Titanic and Revolutionary Road.

items.question: Which films feature both Christian Bale and Amy Adams?
items.ground_truth_answer: They appear together in The Fighter, American Hustle, and Vice.

items.question: Which films feature both George Clooney and Brad Pitt?
items.ground_truth_answer: They co-star in Ocean's Eleven, Ocean's Twelve, Ocean's Thirteen, and Burn After Reading.

items.question: Which Quentin Tarantino films star Uma Thurman?
items.ground_truth_answer: Uma Thurman stars in Pulp Fiction, Kill Bill: Vol. 1, and Kill Bill: Vol. 2.

items.question: Which Stanley Kubrick films focus on war or the military?
items.ground_truth_answer: Paths of Glory, Full Metal Jacket, and Dr. Strangelove are major Kubrick films centered on war and military institutions.
ground_truth_movies: Dr. Strangelove or: How I Learned to Stop Worrying and Love the Bomb

items.question: Which films make up Park Chan-wook's Vengeance Trilogy?
items.ground_truth_answer: The trilogy consists of Sympathy for Mr. Vengeance, Oldboy, and Lady Vengeance.

items.question: Compare organized crime in The Godfather and GoodFellas
items.ground_truth_answer: The Godfather presents crime as dynastic power and family obligation, while GoodFellas emphasizes volatile street-level ambition and betrayal.

items.question: How do Alien and The Thing create horror from isolation?
items.ground_truth_answer: Alien traps a crew with a hidden predator in space, while The Thing uses Antarctic confinement and uncertainty over who remains human.

items.question: Compare the false realities in The Matrix and The Truman Show
items.ground_truth_answer: The Matrix depicts a machine simulation hiding human enslavement, while The Truman Show presents one man's life as a manufactured television set.

items.question: How are ambition and perfectionism portrayed in Whiplash and Black Swan?
items.ground_truth_answer: Both link artistic excellence to bodily and psychological harm, with Whiplash focused on music and Black Swan on ballet.

items.question: Compare communication with aliens in Arrival and Contact
items.ground_truth_answer: Arrival builds communication through linguistics and nonlinear time, while Contact uses scientific signals and a journey shaped by faith and evidence.

items.question: How do Her and Ex Machina differ in their treatment of artificial intelligence?
items.ground_truth_answer: Her treats AI intimacy as an evolving relationship, while Ex Machina frames it through captivity, manipulation, and a test of consciousness.

items.question: Compare the portrayal of war in Apocalypse Now and Full Metal Jacket
items.ground_truth_answer: Apocalypse Now presents war as a surreal descent into madness, while Full Metal Jacket separates dehumanizing training from urban combat.

items.question: How do 12 Angry Men and Anatomy of a Fall explore reasonable doubt?
items.ground_truth_answer: 12 Angry Men focuses on jurors challenging assumptions, while Anatomy of a Fall leaves truth unstable through conflicting testimony and interpretation.

items.question: Compare nature and industrialization in Spirited Away and Princess Mononoke
items.ground_truth_answer: Both criticize human greed, but Spirited Away uses a spirit bathhouse while Princess Mononoke stages an open conflict between industry and the forest.

items.question: Compare ambition in The Social Network and Steve Jobs
items.ground_truth_answer: Both portray brilliant technology leaders whose drive produces innovation while damaging their closest relationships.

items.question: How do Moonlight and Boyhood portray growing up?
items.ground_truth_answer: Moonlight uses three actors and focused chapters to examine identity, while Boyhood follows one actor continuously across twelve years.

items.question: Compare the social criticism in Get Out and Parasite
items.ground_truth_answer: Get Out uses racial horror and liberal hypocrisy, while Parasite uses class conflict and spatial inequality; both turn social systems into suspense.

items.question: How do Blade Runner and The Matrix question what makes someone human?
items.ground_truth_answer: Blade Runner blurs the boundary between humans and replicants, while The Matrix questions whether lived experience remains meaningful inside a simulation.

items.question: Compare hope and injustice in The Shawshank Redemption and The Green Mile
items.ground_truth_answer: Both use prison settings and compassionate inmates, but Shawshank centers on endurance and escape while The Green Mile adds supernatural tragedy.

items.question: How does artistic ambition differ in La La Land and Whiplash?
items.ground_truth_answer: La La Land balances ambition against romance and compromise, while Whiplash portrays achievement through abusive discipline and obsession.

items.question: Compare memory in Memento and Eternal Sunshine of the Spotless Mind
items.ground_truth_answer: Memento treats memory loss as a mystery and tool of self-deception, while Eternal Sunshine explores deliberately erased romantic memories.
ground_truth_movies: Eternal Sunshine of the Spotless Mind

items.question: Compare class hierarchy in Mad Max: Fury Road and Snowpiercer
items.ground_truth_answer: Both visualize oppressive class systems through controlled resources, but Fury Road uses a desert convoy while Snowpiercer uses a vertically ordered train.

items.question: How do No Country for Old Men and Fargo portray crime?
items.ground_truth_answer: Both use regional landscapes and dark humor, but No Country is fatalistic and austere while Fargo stresses ordinary decency amid absurd violence.

items.question: Compare wilderness survival in The Revenant and Cast Away
items.ground_truth_answer: The Revenant emphasizes revenge and physical brutality in a hostile frontier, while Cast Away focuses on solitude, adaptation, and returning home.

items.question: Compare alienation in Joker and Taxi Driver
items.ground_truth_answer: Both follow isolated unreliable men in decaying cities, with Joker transforming the pattern into a comic-book origin and public spectacle.

items.question: How do Dunkirk and 1917 structure wartime suspense?
items.ground_truth_answer: Dunkirk intercuts three timelines across land, sea, and air, while 1917 simulates a continuous journey through enemy territory.

items.question: Compare Greta Gerwig's Lady Bird and Little Women
items.ground_truth_answer: Both center ambitious young women and family bonds, but Lady Bird is a contemporary coming-of-age story while Little Women adapts a period novel.

items.question: How do The Irishman and GoodFellas differ in their view of gangster life?
items.ground_truth_answer: GoodFellas captures the speed and seduction of criminal ascent, while The Irishman looks back on crime through age, regret, and isolation.

items.question: Compare the desert epics Dune and Lawrence of Arabia
items.ground_truth_answer: Both use immense desert landscapes and outsider messiah narratives, while Dune translates those ideas into science fiction and ecological prophecy.

items.question: How do Inception and Paprika depict shared dream worlds?
items.ground_truth_answer: Both enter layered collective dreams and blur reality, with Inception framing dreams as a heist and Paprika as fluid psychological transformation.

items.question: Compare the horror and social themes of Us and Get Out
items.ground_truth_answer: Get Out targets racial exploitation through intimate conspiracy, while Us uses doubles and an underground society to examine privilege and exclusion.

items.question: How do Ratatouille and Chef connect cooking with creativity?
items.ground_truth_answer: Both portray cooking as personal expression and renewal, with Ratatouille using an outsider fairy tale and Chef emphasizing independence and family.

items.question: Compare investigative journalism in Spotlight and All the President's Men
items.ground_truth_answer: Both emphasize patient reporting and institutional resistance, covering the Catholic Church abuse scandal and Watergate respectively.

items.question: How do WALL·E and Interstellar portray environmental collapse?
items.ground_truth_answer: WALL·E uses animation and consumer satire to show an abandoned Earth, while Interstellar treats ecological failure as the impetus for space migration.
